requirement: The system shall provide a web-based user interface accessible via standard browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari.
Section Name: The system shall provide a web-based user interface accessible via standard browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari.

requirement: The system shall support user authentication using username and password credentials stored in the central identity provider.
Section Name: The system shall support user authentication using username and password credentials stored in the central identity provider.

requirement: The system shall validate that the password contains a minimum of 12 characters including at least one uppercase letter, one digit, and one special character.
Section Name: The system shall validate that the password contains a minimum of 12 characters including at least one uppercase letter, one digit, and one special character.

requirement: The system shall reject passwords that match any of the user's previous 12 passwords.
Section Name: The system shall reject passwords that match any of the user's previous 12 passwords.

requirement: The password history check shall be performed server-side before the new password hash is stored.
Section Name: The password history check shall be performed server-side before the new password hash is stored.

requirement: The bcrypt cost factor shall be configurable by the system administrator via the security configuration interface without requiring a system restart.
Section Name: The bcrypt cost factor shall be configurable by the system administrator via the security configuration interface without requiring a system restart.

requirement: The system shall verify that the bcrypt cost factor results in a hash computation time of at least 250ms on the target server hardware before accepting a configuration change.
Section Name: The system shall verify that the bcrypt cost factor results in a hash computation time of at least 250ms on the target server hardware before accepting a configuration change.

requirement: The system shall store only the hash of previous passwords using bcrypt with a minimum cost factor of 12.
Section Name: The system shall store only the hash of previous passwords using bcrypt with a minimum cost factor of 12.

requirement: The system shall reject passwords that contain the user's first name, last name, or username as a substring.
Section Name: The system shall reject passwords that contain the user's first name, last name, or username as a substring.

requirement: The substring check shall be case-insensitive and shall apply to both the full name and each component of a hyphenated name.
Section Name: The substring check shall be case-insensitive and shall apply to both the full name and each component of a hyphenated name.

requirement: The substring matching algorithm shall normalise Unicode characters to NFC form before performing the comparison.
Section Name: The substring matching algorithm shall normalise Unicode characters to NFC form before performing the comparison.

requirement: The system shall enforce password expiry after 90 days and prompt the user to set a new password upon next login.
Section Name: The system shall enforce password expiry after 90 days and prompt the user to set a new password upon next login.

requirement: The password expiry notification shall be sent by email 14 days, 7 days, and 1 day before the expiry date.
Section Name: The password expiry notification shall be sent by email 14 days, 7 days, and 1 day before the expiry date.

requirement: Password expiry notification emails shall be sent by the background notification service within a 1-hour window of the scheduled notification time.
Section Name: Password expiry notification emails shall be sent by the background notification service within a 1-hour window of the scheduled notification time.

requirement: The system shall lock the user account after five consecutive failed authentication attempts within a 10-minute window.
Section Name: The system shall lock the user account after five consecutive failed authentication attempts within a 10-minute window.

requirement: The system shall record each failed authentication attempt in the audit log with timestamp, source IP address, and username.
Section Name: The system shall record each failed authentication attempt in the audit log with timestamp, source IP address, and username.

requirement: The audit log entry for failed authentication shall be written before the response is returned to the client.
Section Name: The audit log entry for failed authentication shall be written before the response is returned to the client.

requirement: The system shall require administrator action or a verified password reset email to unlock a locked account.
Section Name: The system shall require administrator action or a verified password reset email to unlock a locked account.

requirement: The password reset email shall contain a single-use token that expires after 60 minutes.
Section Name: The password reset email shall contain a single-use token that expires after 60 minutes.

requirement: The system shall send an email notification to the registered address when an account lockout event occurs.
Section Name: The system shall send an email notification to the registered address when an account lockout event occurs.

requirement: The email notification shall include the timestamp of the lockout event and the source IP address of the last failed attempt.
Section Name: The email notification shall include the timestamp of the lockout event and the source IP address of the last failed attempt.

requirement: The system shall enforce a session timeout of 30 minutes of inactivity, after which the user shall be required to re-authenticate.
Section Name: The system shall enforce a session timeout of 30 minutes of inactivity, after which the user shall be required to re-authenticate.

requirement: The system shall display a countdown warning 5 minutes before the session timeout threshold is reached.
Section Name: The system shall display a countdown warning 5 minutes before the session timeout threshold is reached.

requirement: The countdown warning shall appear as a modal dialog that does not obstruct the user's current work.
Section Name: The countdown warning shall appear as a modal dialog that does not obstruct the user's current work.

requirement: The system shall provide a 'Stay Logged In' button that resets the session timer when clicked.
Section Name: The system shall provide a 'Stay Logged In' button that resets the session timer when clicked.

requirement: The 'Stay Logged In' action shall be logged in the session audit trail with the timestamp of the extension.
Section Name: The 'Stay Logged In' action shall be logged in the session audit trail with the timestamp of the extension.

requirement: The system shall display the currently authenticated username and role in the application header at all times.
Section Name: The system shall display the currently authenticated username and role in the application header at all times.

requirement: The system shall display the user's assigned role using the role name as defined in the identity provider group membership.
Section Name: The system shall display the user's assigned role using the role name as defined in the identity provider group membership.

requirement: The system shall provide a requirements management module that supports the creation, editing, and deletion of requirements artefacts.
Section Name: The system shall provide a requirements management module that supports the creation, editing, and deletion of requirements artefacts.

requirement: The system shall allow authorised users to create new requirements using a structured form with mandatory and optional fields.
Section Name: The system shall allow authorised users to create new requirements using a structured form with mandatory and optional fields.

requirement: The requirements creation form shall include the fields: Title, Description, Priority, Verification Method, and Status.
Section Name: The requirements creation form shall include the fields: Title, Description, Priority, Verification Method, and Status.

requirement: The Priority field shall be an enumeration with values: Critical, High, Medium, Low.
Section Name: The Priority field shall be an enumeration with values: Critical, High, Medium, Low.

requirement: The system shall prevent the user from saving a requirement with a Verification Method of 'Test' if Status is 'Approved' and no test case is linked.
Section Name: The system shall prevent the user from saving a requirement with a Verification Method of 'Test' if Status is 'Approved' and no test case is linked.

requirement: The blocking rule described in 2.1.1.1.1 shall be configurable per project by the project administrator.
Section Name: The blocking rule described in 2.1.1.1.1 shall be configurable per project by the project administrator.

requirement: When the blocking rule prevents saving, the system shall display a specific error message identifying the missing test case link.
Section Name: When the blocking rule prevents saving, the system shall display a specific error message identifying the missing test case link.

requirement: The Verification Method field shall be an enumeration with values: Test, Analysis, Inspection, Demonstration.
Section Name: The Verification Method field shall be an enumeration with values: Test, Analysis, Inspection, Demonstration.

requirement: Changes to the Verification Method after a requirement is in 'Approved' status shall require a second user to confirm the change.
Section Name: Changes to the Verification Method after a requirement is in 'Approved' status shall require a second user to confirm the change.

requirement: The Status field shall be an enumeration with values: Draft, Under Review, Approved, Deprecated.
Section Name: The Status field shall be an enumeration with values: Draft, Under Review, Approved, Deprecated.

requirement: The system shall generate a unique identifier for each new requirement in the format REQ-NNNN where NNNN is a zero-padded integer.
Section Name: The system shall generate a unique identifier for each new requirement in the format REQ-NNNN where NNNN is a zero-padded integer.

requirement: The unique identifier sequence shall be maintained per project and shall not be reused even if a requirement is deleted.
Section Name: The unique identifier sequence shall be maintained per project and shall not be reused even if a requirement is deleted.

requirement: The system shall record the creating user and creation timestamp as immutable attributes of the requirement.
Section Name: The system shall record the creating user and creation timestamp as immutable attributes of the requirement.

requirement: The system shall support versioning of requirements such that each modification creates a new version and the full change history is retained.
Section Name: The system shall support versioning of requirements such that each modification creates a new version and the full change history is retained.

requirement: Each requirement version shall store the complete attribute set, the modifying user, and the modification timestamp.
Section Name: Each requirement version shall store the complete attribute set, the modifying user, and the modification timestamp.

requirement: The version history shall be accessible from the requirement detail view via a 'Version History' tab.
Section Name: The version history shall be accessible from the requirement detail view via a 'Version History' tab.

requirement: The system shall provide a side-by-side diff view comparing any two versions of a requirement.
Section Name: The system shall provide a side-by-side diff view comparing any two versions of a requirement.

requirement: The diff view shall highlight added text in green and removed text in red with strikethrough formatting.
Section Name: The diff view shall highlight added text in green and removed text in red with strikethrough formatting.

requirement: The system shall allow users to add a change justification comment when saving a modified requirement.
Section Name: The system shall allow users to add a change justification comment when saving a modified requirement.

requirement: The system shall provide a search interface that allows users to find requirements by keyword, attribute value, or section number.
Section Name: The system shall provide a search interface that allows users to find requirements by keyword, attribute value, or section number.

requirement: The keyword search shall search within the Title and Description fields and shall support partial word matching.
Section Name: The keyword search shall search within the Title and Description fields and shall support partial word matching.

requirement: The attribute search shall support filtering by Priority, Status, Verification Method, and Assigned Owner.
Section Name: The attribute search shall support filtering by Priority, Status, Verification Method, and Assigned Owner.

requirement: The system shall provide a traceability management module enabling bidirectional links between requirements and other engineering artefacts.
Section Name: The system shall provide a traceability management module enabling bidirectional links between requirements and other engineering artefacts.

requirement: The system shall support the creation of typed traceability links between requirements artefacts and test cases.
Section Name: The system shall support the creation of typed traceability links between requirements artefacts and test cases.

requirement: The supported link types from requirement to test case shall include: Validates, Partially Validates, and Excludes.
Section Name: The supported link types from requirement to test case shall include: Validates, Partially Validates, and Excludes.

requirement: The system shall mark a link as Suspect when the source requirement is modified after the link is created.
Section Name: The system shall mark a link as Suspect when the source requirement is modified after the link is created.

requirement: The Suspect flag shall persist until a user explicitly clears it after reviewing the link validity.
Section Name: The Suspect flag shall persist until a user explicitly clears it after reviewing the link validity.

requirement: Clearing a Suspect flag shall require the user to enter a justification comment of at least 20 characters.
Section Name: Clearing a Suspect flag shall require the user to enter a justification comment of at least 20 characters.

requirement: The justification comment for Suspect flag clearance shall be visible in the link audit trail without requiring special permissions.
Section Name: The justification comment for Suspect flag clearance shall be visible in the link audit trail without requiring special permissions.

requirement: The system shall record the user and timestamp when a Suspect flag is cleared in the link audit trail.
Section Name: The system shall record the user and timestamp when a Suspect flag is cleared in the link audit trail.

requirement: The system shall notify the link owner by email when a Suspect flag is set on a link they own.
Section Name: The system shall notify the link owner by email when a Suspect flag is set on a link they own.

requirement: The Suspect notification email shall include a direct link to the affected traceability link in the system.
Section Name: The Suspect notification email shall include a direct link to the affected traceability link in the system.

requirement: The system shall display the verification status of all linked test cases in the requirement detail view.
Section Name: The system shall display the verification status of all linked test cases in the requirement detail view.

requirement: The verification status summary shall show counts of Passed, Failed, Not Run, and Blocked test cases linked to the requirement.
Section Name: The verification status summary shall show counts of Passed, Failed, Not Run, and Blocked test cases linked to the requirement.

requirement: The system shall support the creation of typed traceability links between requirements artefacts and design artefacts.
Section Name: The system shall support the creation of typed traceability links between requirements artefacts and design artefacts.

requirement: The supported link types from requirement to design artefact shall include: Satisfies, Derived From, and Conflicts With.
Section Name: The supported link types from requirement to design artefact shall include: Satisfies, Derived From, and Conflicts With.

requirement: The system shall generate a Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) report covering all requirements in a selected module.
Section Name: The system shall generate a Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) report covering all requirements in a selected module.

requirement: The RTM report shall include columns for: Requirement ID, Title, Priority, Verification Method, Linked Test Cases, and Test Status.
Section Name: The RTM report shall include columns for: Requirement ID, Title, Priority, Verification Method, Linked Test Cases, and Test Status.

requirement: The RTM shall include a summary row at the end showing total requirement count and percentage with full test coverage.
Section Name: The RTM shall include a summary row at the end showing total requirement count and percentage with full test coverage.

requirement: The RTM report shall be exportable in CSV and PDF formats.
Section Name: The RTM report shall be exportable in CSV and PDF formats.

requirement: The system shall provide a baseline management module supporting the creation and retrieval of immutable requirement snapshots.
Section Name: The system shall provide a baseline management module supporting the creation and retrieval of immutable requirement snapshots.

requirement: The system shall allow project administrators to create a named baseline of all requirements in a module at any point in time.
Section Name: The system shall allow project administrators to create a named baseline of all requirements in a module at any point in time.

requirement: The baseline creation process shall capture the complete attribute set of every requirement in the module at the moment of creation.
Section Name: The baseline creation process shall capture the complete attribute set of every requirement in the module at the moment of creation.

requirement: The baseline creation shall be atomic — either all requirements are captured or none are, with no partial baseline state.
Section Name: The baseline creation shall be atomic — either all requirements are captured or none are, with no partial baseline state.

requirement: If baseline creation fails due to a system error, the system shall log the failure, notify the administrator, and ensure no partial baseline record is stored.
Section Name: If baseline creation fails due to a system error, the system shall log the failure, notify the administrator, and ensure no partial baseline record is stored.

requirement: The atomicity guarantee shall be implemented using a database transaction that is rolled back in its entirety on any failure.
Section Name: The atomicity guarantee shall be implemented using a database transaction that is rolled back in its entirety on any failure.

requirement: Each baseline shall be assigned a unique name, a version number, and a creation timestamp.
Section Name: Each baseline shall be assigned a unique name, a version number, and a creation timestamp.

requirement: The version number shall follow semantic versioning format MAJOR.MINOR where MAJOR increments on release baselines and MINOR on interim baselines.
Section Name: The version number shall follow semantic versioning format MAJOR.MINOR where MAJOR increments on release baselines and MINOR on interim baselines.

requirement: The system shall prevent the creation of a new baseline with the same name as an existing baseline in the same project.
Section Name: The system shall prevent the creation of a new baseline with the same name as an existing baseline in the same project.

requirement: The system shall prevent modification of any requirement artefact that is part of a locked baseline.
Section Name: The system shall prevent modification of any requirement artefact that is part of a locked baseline.

requirement: An attempt to modify a requirement in a locked baseline shall return an error message identifying the baseline name and the locked requirement ID.
Section Name: An attempt to modify a requirement in a locked baseline shall return an error message identifying the baseline name and the locked requirement ID.

requirement: The system shall provide a baseline comparison view showing differences between two selected baselines of the same module.
Section Name: The system shall provide a baseline comparison view showing differences between two selected baselines of the same module.

requirement: The baseline comparison shall highlight requirements that were added, removed, or modified between the two selected baselines.
Section Name: The baseline comparison shall highlight requirements that were added, removed, or modified between the two selected baselines.

requirement: For modified requirements, the comparison shall show the changed attribute values with old and new values presented side-by-side.
Section Name: For modified requirements, the comparison shall show the changed attribute values with old and new values presented side-by-side.

requirement: The comparison view shall allow the user to filter to show only added, only removed, or only modified requirements.
Section Name: The comparison view shall allow the user to filter to show only added, only removed, or only modified requirements.

requirement: The system shall provide an import module supporting the ingestion of requirements from external document formats.
Section Name: The system shall provide an import module supporting the ingestion of requirements from external document formats.

requirement: The system shall support the import of requirements from Microsoft Word documents in .docx format.
Section Name: The system shall support the import of requirements from Microsoft Word documents in .docx format.

requirement: The Word import shall recognise Heading 1 through Heading 6 styles as hierarchy level indicators.
Section Name: The Word import shall recognise Heading 1 through Heading 6 styles as hierarchy level indicators.

requirement: A Heading 1 paragraph shall create a Level 1 requirement artefact; a Heading 2 shall create a Level 2 child of the nearest preceding Level 1, and so on.
Section Name: A Heading 1 paragraph shall create a Level 1 requirement artefact; a Heading 2 shall create a Level 2 child of the nearest preceding Level 1, and so on.

requirement: If a Heading level is skipped (e.g., Heading 1 followed directly by Heading 3), the system shall insert a placeholder Level 2 artefact and flag it for review.
Section Name: If a Heading level is skipped (e.g., Heading 1 followed directly by Heading 3), the system shall insert a placeholder Level 2 artefact and flag it for review.

requirement: Placeholder artefacts created for skipped heading levels shall be assigned the name '[AUTO-PLACEHOLDER]' and the status 'Requires Review'.
Section Name: Placeholder artefacts created for skipped heading levels shall be assigned the name '[AUTO-PLACEHOLDER]' and the status 'Requires Review'.

requirement: The system shall generate a post-import report listing all placeholder artefacts created during the import process with their location in the module hierarchy.
Section Name: The system shall generate a post-import report listing all placeholder artefacts created during the import process with their location in the module hierarchy.

requirement: The Word import shall create one requirement artefact per paragraph that is not formatted as a Heading style.
Section Name: The Word import shall create one requirement artefact per paragraph that is not formatted as a Heading style.

requirement: The system shall support the import of requirements from CSV files conforming to the project's defined import template.
Section Name: The system shall support the import of requirements from CSV files conforming to the project's defined import template.

requirement: The CSV import template shall require the columns: Identifier, Artifact Type, Name, Primary Text, and parentBinding.
Section Name: The CSV import template shall require the columns: Identifier, Artifact Type, Name, Primary Text, and parentBinding.

requirement: The parentBinding column shall reference the Identifier value of the parent requirement within the same import file.
Section Name: The parentBinding column shall reference the Identifier value of the parent requirement within the same import file.

requirement: A parentBinding value that references an Identifier not present in the same import file shall be treated as a validation error.
Section Name: A parentBinding value that references an Identifier not present in the same import file shall be treated as a validation error.

requirement: The system shall validate the CSV file against the template before commencing import and shall report all validation errors before processing any row.
Section Name: The system shall validate the CSV file against the template before commencing import and shall report all validation errors before processing any row.

requirement: Validation errors shall be reported in a downloadable error report CSV with columns: Row Number, Column Name, Error Description.
Section Name: Validation errors shall be reported in a downloadable error report CSV with columns: Row Number, Column Name, Error Description.

requirement: The system shall support the import of requirements from ReqIF packages in .reqif and .reqifz formats.
Section Name: The system shall support the import of requirements from ReqIF packages in .reqif and .reqifz formats.

requirement: The ReqIF import shall preserve all custom attributes defined in the ReqIF specification file.
Section Name: The ReqIF import shall preserve all custom attributes defined in the ReqIF specification file.
